name: Chocolate Shortcakes with Red Fruit and Whipped Cream
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup sugar, or more to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Place strawberries in a large bowl, add sugar, and gently toss. Set aside for at least 2 hours.
2. Using a fork, mash one-third of the berries; toss again, then add raspberries and cherries. Let stand for 1 hour.
3. Adjust rack to the lower third of the oven; preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
4. For the dough: Sift flour, cocoa powder, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt into a large bowl. Combine egg, buttermilk, milk, and orange zest; stir to mix. Cut butter into dry ingredients until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Add egg mixture; stir just until it forms a soft, moist, sticky dough.
5. Spoon 1/4-cup mounds of dough about 2 inches apart on the baking sheet. Sprinkle a little sugar over each mound; gently pat dough to 1/2-to 3/4-inch thickness.
6. Bake for about 20 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Cool shortcakes on a wire rack.
7. For topping: Whip cream with sugar and vanilla until soft peaks form.
8. To assemble: Split shortcakes in half. Place bottom halves on dessert plates. Top each with berries and cherries, then with whipped cream. Set top of shortcakes on the whipped cream. If desired, sprinkle with a light dusting of powdered sugar. Serves 12.
